title: eriogonum niveum var. decumbens
intro: Eriogonum niveum var. decumbens, also known as Snowbush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States, belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include soothing irritated skin, reducing inflammation, and protecting against infection, making it a valuable remedy for minor burns, cuts, and scrapes. Herbal preparations of Snowbush can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for topical and internal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
